core.label: C# Fundamentals
core.description: .NET setup, syntax, types, and basic program structure.

topics.label: .NET SDK & Project Setup
topics.description: Install the .NET SDK, create projects with dotnet new, and understand the project structure. The SDK includes everything needed.

topics.label: Hello World & Top-Level Statements
topics.description: Write your first program using top-level statements (C# 9+). Minimal boilerplate to get started.

topics.label: Value Types & Reference Types
topics.description: int, double, bool, char, struct vs class, and nullable types. The value/reference distinction is fundamental in C#.

topics.label: Strings & Interpolation
topics.description: string methods, string interpolation ($""), verbatim strings (@""), and raw string literals. Strings are immutable in C#.

topics.label: Operators & Expressions
topics.description: Arithmetic, comparison, logical, null-coalescing (??), and null-conditional (?.) operators.

topics.label: Control Flow
topics.description: if/else, switch expressions, for, foreach, while, do-while. Switch expressions are a modern C# feature.

topics.label: Arrays & Collections
topics.description: Arrays, List<T>, Dictionary<TKey,TValue>, and HashSet<T>. Generic collections are type-safe and performant.

topics.label: Methods & Parameters
topics.description: Methods with ref, out, in, params, and optional parameters. C# offers flexible parameter passing.

topics.label: Namespaces & Using
topics.description: Organize code with namespaces and file-scoped namespaces. using directives and global usings simplify imports.

topics.label: Nullable Reference Types
topics.description: Enable nullable context to catch null reference bugs at compile time. A major safety feature in modern C#.

topics.label: Classes & Objects
topics.description: Fields, properties, constructors, and methods. Classes are the core building block of C# applications.

topics.label: Properties & Auto-Properties
topics.description: Encapsulate fields with get/set accessors and init-only setters. Properties are idiomatic C# for data access.

topics.label: Inheritance & virtual/override
topics.description: Derive classes, override methods, and use base. C# supports single class inheritance.

topics.label: Interfaces
topics.description: Define contracts with interface, default implementations, and explicit interface implementation.

topics.label: Abstract Classes
topics.description: Partial implementations with abstract methods and properties. Use abstract classes when sharing state and behavior.

topics.label: Records
topics.description: Immutable reference types with value equality. Records are ideal for DTOs, events, and configuration.

topics.label: Structs
topics.description: Value types for small, immutable data. Structs avoid heap allocation and GC pressure.

topics.label: Enums
topics.description: Named constants backed by integer types. Use [Flags] for bit-flag enumerations.

topics.label: Sealed Classes
topics.description: Prevent inheritance with sealed. Sealed classes enable compiler optimizations and exhaustive pattern matching.

topics.label: Access Modifiers
topics.description: public, private, protected, internal, and protected internal. Control visibility across assemblies.

core.label: Generics, Delegates & LINQ
core.description: Generic programming, delegates, events, and Language Integrated Query.

topics.label: Generics
topics.description: Create type-safe classes and methods with type parameters. Generics eliminate boxing and casting.

topics.label: Generic Constraints
topics.description: Restrict type parameters with where clauses. Constraints enable calling methods on generic types.

topics.label: Delegates
topics.description: Type-safe function pointers with delegate, Action, Func, and Predicate. Delegates power events and callbacks.

topics.label: Events
topics.description: Publish-subscribe pattern with event keyword. Events decouple producers from consumers.

topics.label: Lambda Expressions
topics.description: Inline anonymous functions with => syntax. Lambdas are used everywhere in LINQ and async code.

topics.label: LINQ Basics
topics.description: Query collections with Where, Select, OrderBy, GroupBy. LINQ unifies data querying across sources.

topics.label: LINQ Advanced
topics.description: Join, Aggregate, SelectMany, query syntax vs method syntax, and deferred execution.

topics.label: Extension Methods
topics.description: Add methods to existing types without modifying them. LINQ itself is built on extension methods.

topics.label: Indexers & Operator Overloading
topics.description: Define [] access on classes and custom operator behavior. Makes domain types feel like built-in types.

core.label: Exception Handling & File I/O
core.description: Error handling, file operations, and working with data.

topics.label: Try/Catch/Finally
topics.description: Handle exceptions with try/catch/finally and exception filters (when). C# exceptions are class-based.

topics.label: Custom Exceptions
topics.description: Create domain-specific exceptions extending Exception. Custom exceptions improve error clarity.

topics.label: Using & IDisposable
topics.description: Deterministic resource cleanup with using statements and IDisposable. Prevents resource leaks.

topics.label: File I/O
topics.description: Read and write files with File, StreamReader, StreamWriter, and FileStream.

topics.label: Working with JSON
topics.description: Serialize and deserialize with System.Text.Json. The built-in JSON library for modern .NET.

topics.label: Logging (ILogger)
topics.description: Structured logging with Microsoft.Extensions.Logging. The standard logging abstraction in .NET.

topics.label: Configuration
topics.description: appsettings.json, environment variables, and IConfiguration. Externalized configuration is a .NET convention.

topics.label: Regular Expressions
topics.description: Pattern matching with System.Text.RegularExpressions. Source generators improve regex performance in .NET 7+.

core.label: Async Programming
core.description: async/await, Task-based patterns, and concurrent programming.

topics.label: async/await Basics
topics.description: Write non-blocking code with async and await. Async is pervasive in .NET for I/O-bound work.

topics.label: Task & Task<T>
topics.description: Represent async operations with Task. Understand Task.Run, Task.WhenAll, and Task.WhenAny.

topics.label: ValueTask
topics.description: Reduce allocations for frequently synchronous async methods. Use ValueTask when the common path is synchronous.

topics.label: Cancellation Tokens
topics.description: Cooperative cancellation with CancellationToken. Essential for graceful shutdown and timeout handling.

topics.label: Async Streams (IAsyncEnumerable)
topics.description: Stream data asynchronously with await foreach. Perfect for database cursors and real-time data.

topics.label: Channels
topics.description: Producer-consumer queues for async data flow. Channels are the modern way to communicate between async tasks.

topics.label: Thread Safety & lock
topics.description: Protect shared state with lock, SemaphoreSlim, and concurrent collections.

topics.label: Parallel Programming
topics.description: Parallel.For, Parallel.ForEach, and PLINQ for CPU-bound parallelism.

core.label: Advanced Language Features
core.description: Pattern matching, attributes, reflection, and .NET ecosystem.

topics.label: Pattern Matching
topics.description: is patterns, switch expressions, property patterns, and relational patterns. Pattern matching makes code more expressive.

topics.label: Tuples & Deconstruction
topics.description: Return multiple values with tuples and deconstruct them. Tuples reduce the need for helper classes.

topics.label: Attributes
topics.description: Annotate code with metadata using attributes. Used for serialization, validation, and framework configuration.

topics.label: Reflection
topics.description: Inspect types at runtime with System.Reflection. Used by DI containers, ORMs, and serializers.

topics.label: Dependency Injection
topics.description: Built-in DI with Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection. DI is fundamental to ASP.NET Core and modern .NET.

topics.label: NuGet Packages
topics.description: Manage dependencies with NuGet. Understanding package management is essential for any .NET project.

topics.label: Source Generators
topics.description: Generate code at compile time to eliminate reflection and boilerplate. A powerful metaprogramming feature.

topics.label: Span<T> & Memory<T>
topics.description: High-performance memory access without allocations. Essential for performance-critical code paths.

core.label: Testing & DevOps
core.description: Unit testing, debugging, and deployment practices.

topics.label: xUnit
topics.description: Write tests with [Fact], [Theory], and assertions. xUnit is the most popular .NET testing framework.

topics.label: NUnit
topics.description: Alternative testing framework with [Test] and [TestCase]. Widely used in enterprise .NET projects.

topics.label: Moq & NSubstitute
topics.description: Mock dependencies for isolated tests. Moq is the most popular .NET mocking library.

topics.label: Integration Testing
topics.description: Test full request pipelines with WebApplicationFactory. Catches bugs that unit tests miss.

topics.label: Code Coverage
topics.description: Measure test coverage with coverlet. Coverage reports reveal untested paths.

topics.label: Debugging in Visual Studio / Rider
topics.description: Breakpoints, watch windows, and diagnostic tools. Effective debugging saves hours of guessing.

topics.label: dotnet CLI
topics.description: Build, run, test, publish, and manage packages from the command line. The CLI is the backbone of .NET tooling.

topics.label: Publishing & Deployment
topics.description: dotnet publish, self-contained apps, trimming, and AOT compilation. Understand deployment options for production.
